claim_audit_results.claim_summary: MAESTRO addresses all four mandatory call scope areas (SR-02 planning/reasoning, SR-03 memory, SR-04 tool integration, SR-05 multi-agent frameworks) in an integrated architecture
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json: SR-02 (robust planning, reasoning, search), SR-03 (memory and state management), SR-04 (external tools and APIs, mandatory), SR-05 (at least one research area including multi-agent frameworks) — all four confirmed present with mandatory: true; consistent with proposal claim

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: OBJ-1 target: ≥40% plan success rate improvement over LLM baselines (ReAct, Reflexion) on WebArena, SWE-bench, GAIA for tasks requiring more than 10 sequential steps
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json: OBJ-1.measurable_target — '≥40% improvement in plan success rate over pure LLM-based agent baselines (ReAct, Reflexion) on standardised agent benchmarks (WebArena, SWE-bench, GAIA) for tasks requiring >10 sequential steps' — direct verbatim match

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: OBJ-2 target: ≥30% improvement in task coherence and factual consistency over context-window-only baselines in sequences exceeding 50 agent steps
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json: OBJ-2.measurable_target — '≥30% improvement in task coherence and factual consistency over context-window-only baselines in interaction sequences exceeding 50 agent steps, measured on MAESTRO's long-horizon evaluation suite' — direct match

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: OBJ-3 target: effective coordination of ≥5 heterogeneous agents, ≥25% joint task improvement over independent baselines, formal mathematical proof of protocol convergence
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json: OBJ-3.measurable_target — 'Demonstrate effective coordination of ≥5 heterogeneous agents on shared tasks, with ≥25% improvement in joint task completion over independent agent baselines, and formal proof of protocol convergence' — direct match

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: OBJ-8 target: ≥30 tools/APIs, ≥95% invocation success rate, ≤5s mean recovery time from tool failure, 100% provenance coverage
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json: OBJ-8.measurable_target — 'Typed tool registry supporting ≥30 external tools/APIs across all three demonstrator domains; tool invocation success rate ≥95% with verified output validation; mean recovery time from tool failure ≤5s; full provenance traces for 100% of tool invocations' — direct match

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Demonstrator targets: OBJ-4 (≤10% vs. clinicians on 500 cases), OBJ-5 (≥15% defect reduction, ≥10% energy reduction), OBJ-6 (≥20% disruption recovery improvement)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json: OBJ-4.measurable_target (500 de-identified clinical cases, within 10% of clinician performance), OBJ-5.measurable_target (≥15% defect reduction, ≥10% energy reduction, 3-month operational period), OBJ-6.measurable_target (≥20% improvement in disruption recovery time) — all three targets directly confirmed

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Three disconnected state-of-the-art AI research communities (LLM-based agents, classical planning/MAS, memory-augmented neural systems); MAESTRO uniquely co-designs across all three
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/project_brief/strategic_positioning.md: 'Positioning Against the State of the Art' section explicitly identifies three communities (LLM-based agents e.g. ReAct/AutoGPT; classical planning and MAS; memory-augmented neural systems) and states 'MAESTRO's distinctive contribution is the co-design of these three capabilities into a unified architecture' — claim directly matches source

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: WP5 healthcare benchmark (T5-01, NIHS lead) will address sex/gender stratification per NIHS's established clinical research ethics review infrastructure
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: WP5 T5-01 ('Clinical use case definition and diagnostic benchmark design', responsible_partner: NIHS) confirmed; docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/capabilities.json: NIHS managerial_capabilities includes 'Established clinical research ethics review infrastructure'. Derivation: NIHS holds established ethics review infrastructure (confirmed) → ethics review infrastructure governs patient cohort composition in line with applicable clinical research standards → cohort composition standards address sex/gender stratification where clinically relevant. The sex/gender stratification claim is a logical derivation from NIHS's confirmed ethics infrastructure, not a directly stated project fact.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Section B.1.1 states MAESTRO pursues 'seven specific, measurable, and achievable objectives' and enumerates OBJ-1, OBJ-2, OBJ-3, OBJ-8, OBJ-4, OBJ-5, OBJ-6 — omitting OBJ-7
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json contains 8 objectives: OBJ-1, OBJ-2, OBJ-3, OBJ-4, OBJ-5, OBJ-6, OBJ-7 ('Open framework release and ecosystem contribution', responsible_partner: BAL, target_month: 48), OBJ-8. Proposal text enumerates only 7 objectives and explicitly states 'seven'. OBJ-7 content appears in B.1.2 under open science practices but is not numbered as an objective.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Section text claim of 'seven' objectives contradicts Tier 3 source: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/objectives.json contains 8 objectives (OBJ-1 through OBJ-8). OBJ-7 ('Open framework release and ecosystem contribution', responsible_partner: BAL) is omitted from the enumerated objectives list in B.1.1, creating a count discrepancy and an unattributed omission of a Tier 3 project fact. Also flagged in assembled_draft.json consistency_log CC-01.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Standardisation pathway: D4-01 submitted as formal standardisation proposal to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE by M48 (KPI-08)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/architecture_inputs/impacts.json: IMP-6 confirms standardisation submission as an impact indicator ('≥1 standardisation proposal submitted to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE') and links D4-01 as the linked deliverable. However, docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: WP4 D4-01 is 'Multi-agent coordination protocol specification', type: report, due_month: 18 — not a standardisation submission and not due at M48. The impact section equates D4-01 with a 'formal standardisation proposal by M48' which contradicts D4-01's M18 due date and 'protocol specification' purpose in the authoritative WP structure.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Contradiction between proposal text and Tier 4 source: the impact section describes D4-01 as a 'formal standardisation proposal...to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE by M48' but docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json defines D4-01 as 'Multi-agent coordination protocol specification' (type: report, due_month: 18, responsible_partner: CERIA). Both the deliverable purpose and the due month are inconsistent. This inconsistency is confirmed in assembled_draft.json consistency_log CC-04. Pre-existing claim_status 'confirmed' cannot be sustained given this direct contradiction with a named Tier 4 source.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Impact B.2.1 and B.2.2 describe D4-01 as a 'formal standardisation proposal' submitted 'to ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC 42 or IEEE by M48', characterizing it as the standardisation submission deliverable
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: WP4 D4-01 is 'Multi-agent coordination protocol specification', type: report, due_month: 18, responsible_partner: CERIA. The proposal content in B.2.1 and B.2.2 characterizes D4-01 as a standardisation proposal document due M48 — this is directly contradicted by wp_structure.json's authoritative definition of D4-01 as a protocol specification due M18.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Material project fact (deliverable ID, purpose, and due month) contradicted by Tier 4 source: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json defines D4-01 as 'Multi-agent coordination protocol specification' due at month 18. The impact section characterizes the same D4-01 as a 'formal standardisation proposal' due at M48. A deliverable cannot have two different purposes and two different due months simultaneously. The D4-01 deliverable in the impact section is unresolved because its specific characterization as a 'standardisation proposal at M48' is not supported by — and directly contradicts — the authoritative Tier 4 WP structure. Also flagged in assembled_draft.json CC-04.

claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: NIHS description — 'Access to de-identified clinical datasets and established clinical validation pathways' — direct verbatim match with proposal claim; docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/capabilities.json: NIHS infrastructure includes 'De-identified clinical data repository (3M+ patient records, ethics-approved)' and 'Clinical simulation environment for AI system validation' — further confirms

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Ethics-sensitive workstreams governed through WP1 ethics compliance oversight and WP5 clinical validation governance; three ethics categories triggered (Category 8 AI, Category 4 health data, Category 2 human participants)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json: ethics_assessment directly assigns ETHICS-01 (Category 8 AI) governance to ATU (Ethics Compliance Coordinator via WP1 T1-03), ETHICS-02 (health data) governance to Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee (ATU+NIHS), ETHICS-03 (human participants in WP5 T5-03) — all three categories and governance assignments directly stated in the source. Section's prior 'inferred' classification is upgraded to 'confirmed' as governance is directly stated, not derived.

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: 11 implementation risks identified; RISK-10 (framework integration, medium likelihood/high impact) mitigated through API contract definition at M1–M3 and integration checkpoints every 6 months
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json: risk_register contains RISK-01 through RISK-11 (11 risks confirmed); RISK-10 ('The independently developed planning, memory, coordination, and tool orchestration modules may not integrate cleanly', likelihood: medium, impact: high) mitigation: 'Define shared data representations, API contracts...in M1–M3. Conduct integration checkpoints every 6 months.' — exact match with proposal claim

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Three governance bodies: General Assembly (all 8 partners, biannual), Project Management Committee (all 8 partners, quarterly), Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee (ATU and NIHS, minimum biannual)
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json: governance_matrix — 'General Assembly' (composition: all 8 partners, meeting_frequency: 'Biannual'), 'Project Management Committee' (composition: all 8 partners, meeting_frequency: 'Quarterly'), 'Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee' (composition: ATU+NIHS, meeting_frequency: 'As needed, with a minimum of biannual convening') — all three bodies, compositions, and frequencies directly confirmed

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Demonstrator sectors: healthcare (WP5-NIHS, call-identified), advanced manufacturing (WP6-ISIA, call-identified), logistics transfer (WP7-ELI, cross-sector) with architectural transferability mapping to in-vehicle autonomous driving
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase3_wp_design/wp_structure.json: WP5 ('Healthcare Demonstrator — Clinical Decision Support', lead: NIHS), WP6 ('Manufacturing Demonstrator — Process Optimisation', lead: ISIA), WP7 ('Logistics Transfer Demonstrator — Cross-Sector Generalisation', lead: ELI, objective: 'Confirm integrated architecture transferability beyond primary call sectors') — all three demonstrators, leads, and cross-sector transfer framing confirmed; in-vehicle mapping referenced in WP7 objectives as 'beyond primary call sectors'

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: TEF (Testing and Experiment Facility) linkage per CC-07 within WP8/FIIT scope
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json: CC-07 ('Projects selected in this topic should link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs. Results may be validated in the Testing and Experiment Facilities', source_section: call_entry.scope); docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json: MGR-WPL-WP8 responsibilities include 'Coordination with Testing and Experiment Facilities per CC-07 (call_constraints.json)' — both constraint and WP8 implementation directly confirmed

claim_audit_results.claim_summary: Implementation B.3.2 refers to ELI as 'EuroLog International' (omitting legal form suffix 'AG')
claim_audit_results.source_ref: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json: ELI.legal_name = 'EuroLog International AG' — the authoritative Tier 3 legal name includes the Swiss legal form suffix 'AG'. Implementation section B.3.2 text states 'EuroLog International contributes real-world logistics data' — the legal form suffix 'AG' is absent.
claim_audit_results.flag_reason: Partner legal name in proposal text does not match authoritative Tier 3 source: docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json states ELI.legal_name = 'EuroLog International AG'. The proposal B.3.2 uses 'EuroLog International' (truncated). This is a project-fact claim (partner legal identity) that cannot be assumed — it requires accurate representation of the Tier 3 source. Also flagged in assembled_draft.json consistency_log CC-03.
